a: The app uses Bluetooth beacons placed throughout campus buildings to pinpoint your location. Combined with building floor plans, it provides turn-by-turn directions to any classroom, lab, or facility.

We offer wheelchair-friendly routing (elevators and ramps), audio navigation with adjustable speech rate, high contrast mode, large text options, and full screen reader support for visually impaired users.

q: How do I make my diary entries public?
a: When viewing an entry, you'll see a privacy indicator. Only public entries can receive admin responses. You can toggle this setting when creating or editing an entry.

q: What happens to my audio recordings?
a: Audio recordings are automatically transcribed using speech-to-text technology, making content accessible to all users. The original audio and transcription are stored securely and privately unless you make the entry public.

q: Can admins see my private entries?
a: No. Private entries are visible only to you. Only entries marked as 'Public' can be viewed by campus administrators, and you can chat with them about these entries.

q: How do I enable audio navigation?
a: Go to Profile > Settings > Audio section and toggle 'Audio Navigation' on. You can adjust the speech rate and test the feature. The app will speak directions as you navigate.

q: What if I'm in an unknown location?
a: If the system doesn't recognize your location, the navigation feature might not work as expected.
Yes. All data is encrypted and stored securely. We comply with GDPR regulations. See our GDPR tab for detailed information about data protection and your rights.
For technical support or accessibility assistance, contact campus IT at support@university.edu.
